1 min read
Just now

--

Write a function to find the longest common prefix string amongst an array of strings.
If there is no common prefix, return an empty string "".
class Solution:
def longestCommonPrefix(self, strs: List[str]) -> str:
res=""
for i in range(len(strs[0])):
for s in strs:
if i==len(s) or s[i]!=strs[0][i]:
return res
res+= strs[0][i]
return resExplanation:
Input: strs = ["flower","flow","flight"]
• res = ""
i = 0

• Compare first character of every string:

• "flower"[0] = 'f'

• "flow"[0] = 'f'

• "flight"[0] = 'f'

• All match.

• res = "f"
i = 1

• Compare second character:

• "flower"[1] = 'l'

• "flow"[1] = 'l'

• "flight"[1] = 'l'

• All match.

• res = "fl"
i = 2

• Compare third character:

• "flower"[2] = 'o'

• "flow"[2] = 'o'

• "flight"[2] = 'i' ❌ mismatch

• Return res
Time Complexity:O(n × m) where:

• n = number of strings

• m = length of the first string (or the shortest string in the worst case)
Space Complexity: O(1)
